page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the t2a server family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running on the ARMv8 architecture with Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processors clocked at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 87022.37 and a Redis SET throughput of 61.65 million operations per second. Single-core performance is more modest, with Geekbench single-core scores categorized as weak at 1110.0, and stress-ng single-core performance sitting in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average results, with uncached read speeds of 181.23 GB/sec and write speeds of 78.56 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference tasks represent a performance tradeoff, yielding weak text generation speeds of 2.75 tokens per second on 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-48 leverages the ARM64 architecture to deliver high resource density and strong multi-threaded performance without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. This makes it highly suitable for scale-out workloads. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded applications, Redis caching layers, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose containerized deployments that benefit from dedicated physical cores.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM64-based general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on the Neoverse-N1 architecture and 192 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.0 GHz, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 87022.37 and a high Redis SET throughput of 61.65 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, and the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a highly cost-efficient option for multi-threaded scale-out workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database caching. The system features 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, though it lacks advanced vector extensions.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM64 server featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 192 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core performance and high Redis throughput for scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an ARM64 server with 48 dedicated cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and balanced memory for efficient multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
192 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong multi-core performance with 87022.37 stress-ng score and high Redis throughput
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database caching
